encounter.chiefComplaint: Right ankle injury during soccer — swelling and difficulty bearing weight

clinicalNote.subjective: 11-year-old male presents with right ankle injury sustained during soccer. Reports swelling and difficulty bearing weight. No prior ankle injuries. Guardian present.
clinicalNote.objective: HR 84 bpm, RR 18, Temp 36.7°C, SpO2 99%. Weight 38.5 kg, Height 148 cm. Right ankle: lateral swelling, tenderness over ATFL, no fibula point tenderness. Ottawa ankle rules negative for fracture. X-ray right ankle: no acute fracture or dislocation.
clinicalNote.plan: X-ray right ankle — no fracture. PRICE, ace wrap, crutches partial weight-bearing as tolerated 48-72h then gradual return. NSAIDs as above. Home exercises handout for ROM beginning day 3-5 if improving. RTC if inability to bear weight after 5-7 days, numbness, or recurrent instability.
clinicalNote.patientInstructions: Avoid soccer until pain-free with normal gait and single-leg hop test cleared by clinician.

allergies.reaction: GI upset only — tolerated with food

medications.name: ibuprofen
medications.frequency: every 8 hours with food x 3-5 days

conditions.name: Sprain of right ankle, initial encounter

dietPlan.notes: High-protein diet to support tissue healing post ankle sprain. Standard pediatric macros (age 11).

agentContext.oneLineSummary: 11M acute lateral ankle sprain; XR negative; conservative management and RTP precautions documented.
keyFactsForTools: Growth plate injury risk — XR negative reduces fracture concern but clinical follow-up for persistent symptoms.
NSAID 'allergy' mild GI only — monitor tolerance; currently prescribed with food instructions.
Return-to-play criteria should reference pain-free gait and functional testing.
